title: Teriyaki Chicken Rice Bowls
description: A delicious and easy-to-make teriyaki chicken dish served over rice, perfect for a family meal. This recipe is designed to be kid-friendly and uses minimal pans for easy cleanup.
instructions: In a medium bowl, whisk together soy sauce, mirin, sake (if using), brown sugar, minced garlic, and grated ginger. Set aside half of the sauce for later.
Cut chicken thighs into 1-inch pieces. Add chicken to the bowl with the remaining sauce and marinate for at least 10 minutes (or up to 30 minutes in the refrigerator).

In a large skillet or wok, heat a little oil over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken and cook until browned and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes. Remove chicken from the skillet and set aside.
In the same skillet, add broccoli florets and sliced carrots. Sauté for 3-5 minutes until tender-crisp. If needed, add a splash of water to help steam the vegetables.
While vegetables are cooking, in a small bowl, whisk together cornstarch and 2 tablespoons of water to create a slurry. Add the reserved teriyaki sauce to the skillet with the vegetables.
Bring the sauce to a simmer, then slowly whisk in the cornstarch slurry. Cook, stirring constantly, until the sauce thickens, about 1-2 minutes.
Return the cooked chicken to the skillet and toss to coat evenly with the thickened teriyaki sauce and vegetables.
Serve immediately over cooked rice. Garnish with sesame seeds and sliced green onions, if desired.
tips: For picky eaters, you can finely chop the broccoli and carrots or use other kid-friendly vegetables like peas or corn.
To save time, use pre-cooked rice or cook the rice while the chicken is marinating and cooking.
Adjust the amount of brown sugar to your family's preference for sweetness in the teriyaki sauce.
